generated_parts.label: Avionics Bay Mock Tray
generated_parts.spec: Avionics_bay educational tray with board_standoff, harness_passthrough, connector labels, EMI shield mock, no ordnance or flight computer authority

requirements: Avionics Bay educational tray with board_standoff, harness_passthrough, connector labels, EMI shield mock, no ordnance or flight computer authority
Saturn V-structured, Electron-class 2-stage smallsat launcher demonstrator
university teaching model, labeled cross-sections, transparent inspection windows
Avionics tray with board mounts and harness pass-throughs
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets
Most geometry_ops include args.at coordinates distributed across the local subsystem volume
Uses drill, pocket, boss, channel, pattern_polar, fillet, chamfer — not just boxes
Every part_tree child has 4+ dedicated geometry_ops (body+subtract+interface+finish)
A primary datum feature (flat, bore, or pin) is explicitly named and placed
Adjacent interfaces include fit_class (H7/h6, LC2) and stack_up_mm
cad_brief includes ordered assembly_sequence with tool, torque, datum per step
Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, subtract, pocket, drill
Dimensions match small_launch_vehicle scale: 100-500mm dia, 500-2000mm length (display model)
Wall thickness, hole diameters, fillet radii are physically plausible
Design loads: static display 1g, handling 2g, no flight loads
Respect interfaces from the skeleton
Subsystem scale: 100-500mm dia, 500-2000mm length (display model)
Environment: indoor display, handling loads, educational use

generated_parts.spec: Integration_inspection ring with assembly_datum pads, inspection_window, checklist_callout, connection type labels, load-path closure marks

blueprint.project: Saturn V-structured, Electron-class 2-stage smallsat launcher demonstrator

requirements: Provide visual inspection access to assembly datum
Ensure load path closure from dummy turbopump to assembly datum
Enable checklist verification access
Route electrical harness for subsystem connection
Define interfaces for structural and electrical connections

verify.value: All required fields present and valid.
verify.rule: All fields in the schema are present and correctly formatted.
The mass_est_g value is a numeric grams value.

verify.value: All P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
verify.rule: P0 internal_feature_targets must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops.

verify.value: Interfaces with adjacent subsystems are reciprocal and named.
verify.rule: Interfaces must name exact neighboring subsystem labels with connection_type, DOF, clearance, and required_feature.

verify.value: Visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence for critical joints.
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible locking/preload evidence.
Boundary_conditions must include fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces.

risk.desc: Risk of assembly sequence feasibility issues due to complex interfaces.
risk.mit: Conduct detailed assembly simulation and review.

risk.desc: Risk of tolerance accumulation due to lack of datum continuity.
risk.mit: Ensure datum chain continuity and specify fit_class for interfaces.

risk.desc: Risk of inspection window visibility obstruction.
risk.mit: Verify inspection window placement and size during design review.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion mismatch between materials.
risk.mit: Specify thermal expansion coefficients and conduct thermal analysis.

risk.desc: Risk of overclaiming structural integrity for non-flight components.
risk.mit: Ensure all structural claims are limited to display and educational purposes.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Assembly order evidence, inspection windows, checklist hooks, connection types and load-path closure

physics_paths.description: Path for visual inspection of assembly datum
Load path from dummy turbopump to assembly datum
Access path for checklist verification
Electrical harness path for subsystem connection
